title: Goal Contracts and Observable Completion · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: Turn a vague long-running objective into an executable goal contract. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a measurable goal and done-condition specification. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Write a goal as an outcome rather than an unbounded activity.
Attach observable success measures, boundaries, and evidence requirements.
Separate current objective, durable constraints, and optional next work.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""Bounded agent-loop portfolio. No external effects occur in this browser lab."""

steps.title: Goal Outcome · changed case
steps.instruction:
Use a changed quiz case derived from this module: loops goal contracts. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

content.text: Write a goal as an outcome rather than an unbounded activity. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Goal Hierarchy applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Relate mission, milestone, task, and step without losing the terminal outcome.
